description: Unit: `diffTicketFields` returns only changed field names; ignores unchanged fields; handles nullâ†”value transitions; handles JSONB column equality (per CLAUDE.md JSONB rule).
Unit: `publishTicketUpdate` writes JSON to the correct Redis channel `<prefix>ticket-updates:<tenant>:<ticketId>` with the expected payload shape.
Unit: `publishTicketUpdate` swallows Redis errors (logs, does not throw); caller's update transaction is unaffected.
Integration: a successful `updateTicketWithCache` call results in exactly one Redis publish on the matching channel containing only the fields that actually changed.
Integration: a failed `updateTicketWithCache` (validation error / permission error) results in zero Redis publishes.
Integration: bundled-parent ticket sync-propagation publishes one message per affected child on the child's channel.
Integration: when `LIVE_TICKET_UPDATES_DISABLED=1`, `updateTicketWithCache` succeeds with zero Redis publishes.
Unit: `/api/tickets/:id/live-token` returns 401 when unauthenticated.
Unit: `/api/tickets/:id/live-token` returns 403 when the user lacks read access (e.g., board not visible to client-portal user).
Unit: `/api/tickets/:id/live-token` returns a JWT whose claims include `{tenantId, userId, ticketId}` and `exp - iat â‰¤ 5 min`.
Unit: JWT signed with the wrong secret is rejected by `tenantValidation.js` JWT verification.
Unit: expired JWT is rejected by `tenantValidation.js`.
Unit: JWT for tenant X presented to room `ticket:Y:abc` is rejected.
Unit: JWT for ticket A presented to room `ticket:T:B` is rejected.
Unit: `parseTicketRoom('ticket:tenant1:abc-123')` returns `{tenantId:'tenant1', ticketId:'abc-123'}`; malformed names return null.
Unit: `validateDocumentRoomAccess` continues to bypass `notifications:` rooms unchanged after the ticket-room addition (regression).
Unit: `validateDocumentRoomAccess` continues to handle `document:` rooms unchanged (regression).
Integration (Hocuspocus): a Redis publish to `<prefix>ticket-updates:T:1` triggers a stateless broadcast in room `ticket:T:1`; clients connected to that room receive the message.
Integration (Hocuspocus): a Redis publish for a room with no connected clients is a no-op (no error, no leak).
Integration (Hocuspocus): killing the Redis subscriber connection and reconnecting resumes pattern subscription without restart.
Direct WebSocket probe: connecting with a token for tenant X to room `ticket:Y:*` is rejected at handshake.
Component: `PresenceBar` renders one avatar per unique `userId`, deduping multi-tab.
Component: `PresenceBar` shows tooltip with display name on hover.
Component: documents `CollaborativeEditor` still renders presence correctly after the lift (regression).
Component: `FieldConflictBanner` shows remote value, author display name, and relative timestamp; both buttons are accessible (correct ARIA, focus on Keep yours by default).
Hook: `useTicketLive` requests a token, opens a provider, transitions `connectionStatus` connecting â†’ connected on successful WS handshake.
Hook: `useTicketLive` JWT refresh fires at ~80% of TTL; updated token is passed to the provider.
Hook: refresh-endpoint failure transitions `connectionStatus` to `unavailable` and stops further auto-retries.
Hook: WS disconnect triggers exponential reconnect (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s caps at 30s).
Hook: after 5 failed reconnect attempts, `connectionStatus` becomes `unavailable` and no further retries fire.
Hook: successful reconnect after a drop fires exactly one ticket refetch.
Hook: `setEditingField('status_id')` updates awareness; `setEditingField(null)` clears it.
Component: connection-status indicator hidden when connected; shows 'Live updates offline â€” reconnectingâ€¦' when reconnecting; shows 'Live updates unavailable' when permanently failed.
Component (TicketDetails remote-update routing): no-overlap path â†’ silent refetch; field highlights ~600ms; no toast.
Component: 5 update messages within 200ms collapse into a single refetch (debounce).
Component: non-overlap with unsaved local change â†’ toast '{Name} updated {field}'; local pending change preserved.
Component: same-field overlap with unsaved local change â†’ field freezes, banner appears with remote value/author/timestamp.
Component: Keep yours â†’ banner clears, field unfreezes, local pending value retained, no immediate save fired.
Component: Take theirs â†’ banner clears, field unfreezes, local pending value dropped, field shows remote value (verified after refetch).
Component: focusing status dropdown sets `awareness.editingField='status_id'`; blurring clears it.
Component: when remote awareness has `editingField='priority_id'`, the priority field on this client renders dimmed with '{Name} is editing' caption.
Component: indicator clears within ~2s after the remote user blurs the field.
Component: A and B both focused on status simultaneously â€” each sees the other's indicator; neither field is hard-locked (still selectable).
Component: focusing the title input sets `awareness.editingField='title'`; on a remote client, title shows a caption pill next to the input (no dim); pill clears on blur.
E2E: B saves a title change; A sees the new title within ~500ms without reload (verifies title flows through the same save-time broadcast as dropdown fields).
Component: refetch returning 403 after a remote-update message redirects away from the ticket page.
Component: with feature flag `live-ticket-updates` off, `useTicketLive` does not mount, no token request fires, no presence renders, ticket page behaves identically to today.
E2E: two browsers, two users, one ticket â€” B saves status; A's status updates within 500ms without reload; A's other unsaved fields preserved.
E2E: kill the Hocuspocus container; ticket page still loads; saves still work; offline indicator visible; bring container back; reconnect within ~30s; indicator clears; one refetch fires.
E2E: A focuses status; B sees indicator; A blurs; indicator clears.
E2E: A has unsaved status='On Hold'; B saves status='Resolved'; A sees conflict banner; A clicks Take theirs â†’ field shows 'Resolved'; A's other unsaved fields untouched.
E2E: A has unsaved status='On Hold'; B saves priority='High'; A sees toast '{B's name} updated priority'; status field still shows A's pending 'On Hold'; priority shows 'High'.
E2E: One user opens the ticket in two tabs; presence shows that user once.
E2E (regression): all existing ticket Playwright tests pass unchanged with the live layer enabled.
E2E (regression): all existing ticket Playwright tests pass unchanged with the feature flag off.

Security: a user from tenant X cannot enumerate or join `ticket:Y:*` rooms via direct WS probe even with a token (T021 verifies tenant; this verifies they can't substitute another ticketId within their own tenant either if access is missing).
Security: presence awareness payload contains only userId, displayName, avatarUrl, color, editingField â€” no email, no role, no permissions snapshot.
Security: Redis broadcast payload contains only field names + author metadata + timestamp â€” no field values. Inspect channel during T046.
Performance smoke: in dev, save â†’ remote-client-applied latency P95 â‰¤ 500ms over 50 saves (manual stopwatch acceptable; not a CI gate).
